title: castanopsis rhamnifolia
intro: Castanopsis rhamnifolia, also known as the Taiwan chinquapin, is a species of evergreen tree native to Taiwan and southern China, belonging to the beech family (Fagaceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Castanopsis rhamnifolia include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Castanopsis rhamnifolia is often prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and its bark and leaves can be used to make extracts and tinctures for medicinal purposes.
